task:
Review this final unlock progression for a tile-matching puzzle game called Slominoes. 

GAME CONTEXT:
- 8x8 grid, players place domino-like tiles (2 symbols each) through entry points on board edges
- Matching 3+ same symbols in a row/column scores points and LOCKS those cells
- Locked cells are immune to respins (row/column randomization)
- Players can buy additional respins by spending score (escalating cost: 100, 150, 200...)
- A "run" is levels 1-10, each level has a score threshold to beat
- Later levels have more wall cells and higher thresholds
- ~5-10 minutes per run
- 5 base symbols always available (cherry freq 5, lemon freq 4, bar freq 3, bell freq 2, seven freq 1)
- Players pick 5 symbols from their unlocked pool before each run
- 16 tiles per level, so 32 symbol placements per level

KEY DESIGN REQUIREMENT: We want 100+ runs to unlock everything (8-15 hours). At most 1 unlock per run, usually 0. Each unlock should feel like a significant event, not routine. We want this to drive replayability over dozens to hundreds of runs.

TYPICAL PLAYER STATS (estimated):
- Average score per run: 500-1500 (varies wildly by skill/luck)
- Average levels won per run: 3-5 early, improving to 5-8 with better symbols
- Full run win (beat level 10): rare early, maybe 20-30% once skilled
- Buying respins: 1-3 per run average, some runs 0
- 8-length match (full row/column): very rare, maybe 1 in 20+ runs
- Average locked cells per level: 8-15

FINAL UNLOCK SYSTEM:

Tier 1 — Early game (runs 3-15, 4 unlocks):
- Jam (ML3, score 25, freq 3, on_match: 2x cherry in same row/col): Cumulative score 10,000
- Bomb (ML2, score 20, freq 2, on_match: clear unlocked adjacent + self): Win 15 levels total
- Honey (ML2, score 15, freq 3, on_adjacent_match: +30): Cumulative score 40,000
- Ghost (ML3, score 30, freq 2, passive: no-lock when matched): Create an 8-length match

Tier 2 — Mid game (runs 15-40, 4 unlocks):
- Coral (ML2, score 15, freq 3, on_match: +20 per unique adjacent type): Win 50 levels total
- Egg (ML4, score 30, freq 3, on_match: +3 tiles): Cumulative score 100,000
- Ember (ML2, score 20, freq 2, passive: +40 when respin creates match): Buy 30 respins total (lifetime)
- Compass (ML3, score 5, freq 2, passive: +1 entry spot east/west): Win a full run (beat level 10)

Tier 3 — Late game (runs 40-80, 4 unlocks):
- Apple (ML3, score 25, freq 2, wild_match cherry/lemon + recipe_match fruit salad +200): Score 1500 from cherry matches in a single run
- Magnet (ML3, score 0, freq 3, passive: bell freq +2, seven freq +1): Match bell 3 times in a single level
- Vine (ML3, score 35, freq 2, on_place: replaces walls, on_match: +50): Win 10 full runs
- Oil Can (ML1, score 40, freq 1, on_match: unlock all locked cells in row+col): Have 30+ locked cells at any point in a level

Tier 4 — Endgame (runs 80-150+, 3 unlocks):
- Crown (ML3, score 20, freq 4, passive: +2 selection slots): Use 12 different symbols across all runs
- Banana (ML3, score 20, freq 3, wild_match all fruits + recipe_match grand salad +400): Trigger Fruit Salad in a run (requires Apple)
- Tide (ML3, score 10, freq 2, on_match: +5 per empty cell): Win a full run without buying any respins

POWER RANKINGS (from exhaustive 15,504 loadout simulation):
Compass(1341) > Oil Can(1234) > Bell(1192) > Seven(1150) > Ember(1106) > Apple(1079) > Vine(1029) > Magnet(1002) > Bar(1000) > Banana(998) > Coral(993) > Jam(984) > Lemon(976) > Ghost(975) > Tide(961) > Egg(960) > Cherry(957) > Honey(900) > Bomb(888) > Crown(872)

Top archetype: Fruit deck (cherry+lemon+apple+banana+compass) — 58.7% win, requires Tier 3 + Tier 4 unlocks.
Second archetype: High-value (bell+seven+magnet+compass+ghost) — 52.7% win.

Please evaluate:
1. Given the estimated player stats, do these thresholds actually produce the pacing we want (usually 0 unlocks per run, at most 1)?
2. Are any conditions still too easy or too hard for their tier?
3. Ghost requiring an 8-length match (full row/column) — is this the right difficulty for a Tier 1 unlock? It might take 20+ runs.
4. Does the mix of cumulative vs per-run vs per-level conditions feel right?
5. Is there a dead zone anywhere in the progression where players go too long without an unlock?
6. Any final concerns before we lock this in?
